subtitle: From Gupta Vision to a Thousand Lamps of Learning

panels.shot: sweeping low-angle wide of a vast red-brick monastery complex rising from green plains
panels.action: Kumaragupta the First stands in regal Gupta armor and flowing robes on a low hill, gazing over the newly built brick monasteries and temples of Nalanda, with monks and students moving like ants below.

dialogue.text: Nalanda, in the age of Kumaragupta the First: a monastery sown in Magadha that grew into an ocean of learning for seven hundred years.

i18n.hi: कुमारगुप्त प्रथम के युग में नालंदा: मगध में बोया गया विहार, जो सात सौ साल तक ज्ञान का सागर बना रहा।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of the Gangetic plains at dawn
panels.action: In the soft orange light, the Magadhan countryside stretches with rivers and groves, while a distant Gupta cavalry column rides past a stupa, Kumaragupta the First at its head upon a caparisoned horse.

dialogue.text: In the fifth century, under the Guptas, the Ganga plains glowed with new order, coin and song. Yet beyond temples and conquests, another hunger stirred in Magadha.

i18n.hi: पाँचवीं सदी में, गुप्तों के समय, गंगा का मैदानी इलाका नए नियम, सिक्कों और गीतों से चमक उठा। पर मंदिरों और विजय के आगे, मगध में एक और भूख जागी।

panels.shot: medium close-up three-quarter profile of Kumaragupta in court
panels.action: Kumaragupta the First sits on a carved throne in a pillared hall, wearing a simple yet rich diadem, listening intently to a learned monk and a Brahmin scholar who stand respectfully before him.

dialogue.text: Kumaragupta the First, heir to Chandragupta and Samudragupta, ruled a realm that called itself golden. Poets praised him, merchants paid him, but his advisors spoke of another gift a king could leave.

i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त और समुद्रगुप्त के उत्तराधिकारी कुमारगुप्त प्रथम पर एक ‘सुवर्ण’ साम्राज्य राज करता था। कवि गाते, व्यापारी कर चुकाते, पर मंत्रियों ने एक और विरासत की बात की, जो राजा छोड़ सकता है।

panels.shot: over-the-shoulder shot from behind Kumaragupta towards the scholars
panels.action: The monk gestures toward a palm-leaf manuscript and a simple plan drawn on the floor, while the Brahmin scholar nods, as Kumaragupta leans forward, his fingers resting thoughtfully on the arm of his throne.

dialogue.speaker: kumaragupta_i
dialogue.text: A monastery that welcomes all sharp minds… in Magadha’s very heart?

i18n.hi: मगध के दिल में ऐसा विहार, जहाँ हर तेज दिमाग का स्वागत हो?

panels.shot: high-angle wide of the chosen site at Nalanda as a barren field
panels.action: On an open stretch of land near a small village and an old stupa, Kumaragupta the First stands with officials and architects, tracing lines in the dust as laborers and monks look on, the wind lifting his cloak.

dialogue.text: Near an older shrine in Nalanda, in Magadha, the king chose a site. Copper-plate grants would later remember his gift, naming him as the founder of a new mahavihara.

i18n.hi: मगध के नालंदा में एक प्राचीन देवालय के पास, राजा ने स्थल चुना। ताम्रपत्र भविष्य में उसकी दानभूमि याद करेंगे, उसे नए महाविहार का संस्थापक कहेंगे।

panels.action: Kumaragupta the First, bareheaded now, pours water from a ceremonial vessel onto the earth beside the first brick course, while monks chant and artisans stand ready with baskets of bricks.

dialogue.text: With ritual water and royal seal, he endowed the monastery. Land, revenue, and protection were tied to Nalanda, so that teachers could teach without fear of hunger or tax.

i18n.hi: विधि से जल चढ़ाकर, राजमुद्रा लगा कर उसने विहार को दान दिया। भूमि, आमदनी और सुरक्षा नालंदा से जुड़ गई, ताकि गुरु बिना भूख या कर के डर के पढ़ा सकें।

panels.shot: time-lapse style wide shot of construction
panels.action: A panoramic view shows brick buildings rising over time: monks carry bricks, artisans carve railings, and in the foreground Kumaragupta the First walks once more through the half-built cloisters, inspecting in quiet satisfaction.

dialogue.text: Brick by brick, the red monasteries rose. Cells, lecture halls, shrines and wells formed a city of study, as the Gupta king’s donation took shape in fired clay and stone.

i18n.hi: ईंट पर ईंट, लाल विहार उठे। कक्ष, पाठशालाएँ, देवालय और कुएँ—सब मिलकर एक अध्ययन-नगरी बने, जहाँ गुप्त सम्राट का दान मिट्टी और पत्थर में आकार लेने लगा।

panels.shot: wide top-down schematic-like shot of early Nalanda complex
panels.action: From above, an early Nalanda complex sprawls: quadrangles of monks’ cells around courtyards, a central shrine, tanks glinting with water, and tree-lined paths where robed figures move in quiet lines.

dialogue.text: In time, Nalanda grew beyond a mere monastery. It became a planned mahavihara, a cluster of courts and shrines, where hundreds of residents could live, debate and meditate within its walls.

i18n.hi: धीरे-धीरे नालंदा साधारण विहार से आगे बढ़ा। वह एक योजनाबद्ध महाविहार बना—आँगनों और देवालयों का समूह, जहाँ सैकड़ों निवासी दीवारों के भीतर रह सकें, बहस कर सकें, ध्यान कर सकें।

panels.shot: medium interior shot of a lecture hall
panels.action: Inside a dim brick hall, lit by oil lamps and daylight from a high doorway, a senior monk sits on a raised platform, while rows of students on reed mats listen, palm-leaf manuscripts resting on their knees.

dialogue.text: Here, learning was not a side ritual but the very purpose. Logic, grammar, medicine and mathematics stood beside Buddhist thought, as teachers trained minds to argue as sharply as they prayed.

i18n.hi: यहाँ अध्ययन कोई अतिरिक्त कर्मकांड नहीं, बल्कि लक्ष्य ही लक्ष्य था। तर्क, व्याकरण, चिकित्साशास्त्र और गणित, बौद्ध विचार के साथ खड़े थे, जब गुरु दिमागों को उतना ही तेज तर्क सिखाते, जितना गहन प्रार्थना।

panels.shot: medium shot of a debate in a courtyard
panels.action: In a sunlit courtyard, two monks stand facing each other, one gesturing in measured argument while other residents watch in rapt attention, some smiling, some frowning, a faint dust haze hanging in the air.

dialogue.text: Debate was sport and discipline. A careless argument could bar a student from higher instruction. A clear, fearless answer could open doors to the most guarded teachings of the monastery.

i18n.hi: वाद-विवाद खेल भी था, साधना भी। ढीली दलील पर छात्र को ऊँची शिक्षा से रोका जा सकता था। साफ, निडर जवाब से विहार की सबसे गुप्त शिक्षा के द्वार खुल सकते थे।

panels.shot: wide shot of Dharmaganja library complex exterior
panels.action: A larger, more ornate cluster of buildings stands within the complex, with multi-story brick structures and small towers, monks moving in and out with bundles of palm leaves clutched carefully to their chests.

dialogue.text: In later centuries a vast library grew within Nalanda, remembered as Dharmaganja, the Treasury of the Dharma. Its buildings gained names like Ratnasagara, Ratnodadhi, Ratnaranjaka—the Sea, the Ocean, the Adorner of Jewels.

i18n.hi: सदियों बाद नालंदा के भीतर एक विशाल पुस्तकालय उगा, जिसे धर्मगंज, यानी धर्म का भंडार कहा गया। इसके भवनों के नाम पड़े—रत्नसागर, रत्नोदधि, रत्नरंजक—रत्नों का सागर, महासागर, श्रृंगार।

panels.shot: intimate interior shot inside the library stacks
panels.action: Inside a cool, shadowed room lined with wooden shelves, rows of wrapped palm-leaf manuscripts rest, while two monks carefully copy text by lamplight, their hands steady, their eyes narrowed in concentration.

dialogue.text: Palm leaves inked and bound, texts copied and recited—the library was called the Sea of Gems. It held Buddhist sutras and treatises, but also works on arts, sciences and rival philosophies.

i18n.hi: ताड़-पत्रों पर स्याही, नकलें, पाठ—इस पुस्तकालय को ‘रत्नों का सागर’ कहा गया। इसमें बौद्ध सूत्र और ग्रंथ ही नहीं, कला, विज्ञान और प्रतिद्वंद्वी दर्शन भी रखे थे।

panels.shot: wide shot of the monastery gate with travelers
panels.action: At a large brick gateway, monks welcome new arrivals: some are local youths in simple cloth, others wear foreign robes and carry long staffs, their faces marked by different features and travel-worn expressions.

dialogue.text: Students came not only from nearby villages but from far kingdoms. In later ages, seekers would journey from Kucha, Korea and beyond, drawn by Nalanda’s reputation across Asia.

i18n.hi: विद्यार्थी सिर्फ आसपास के गाँवों से नहीं, दूर-दूर के राज्यों से आए। बाद के युगों में कूचा, कोरिया और उससे भी आगे से साधक नालंदा की एशिया-भर की ख्याति से खिंचे चले आए।

panels.shot: medium shot of a foreign monk in the crowd, evoking Xuanzang
panels.action: A foreign monk with a travel-worn cloak and staff stands amid the crowd in the courtyard, looking up in wonder at the tall brick structures and the bustle of monks around him.

dialogue.text: Centuries after Kumaragupta, the Chinese pilgrim Xuanzang would walk these courtyards. He wrote of thriving monasteries, thousands of residents and famed teachers whose names echoed back to his homeland.

i18n.hi: कुमारगुप्त के बहुत बाद, चीनी यात्री श्वेनज़ांग ने इन्हीं प्रांगणों में कदम रखे। उसने समृद्ध विहारों, हज़ारों निवासियों और ऐसे आचार्यों का वर्णन किया, जिनके नाम चीन तक गूँज उठे।

panels.shot: high-angle shot of densely populated Nalanda at its peak
panels.action: The courtyards, stairways and terraces of Nalanda teem with robed figures: some carrying manuscripts, others sweeping, some meditating under trees; the complex feels like a small city, ordered yet alive.

dialogue.text: Xuanzang spoke of around ten thousand students and two thousand teachers. His numbers may be rounded praise, but they show how Nalanda impressed those who saw it at its height.

i18n.hi: श्वेनज़ांग ने लगभग दस हज़ार छात्रों और दो हज़ार आचार्यों का ज़िक्र किया। शायद आँकड़े बढ़ाए गए हों, पर बताते हैं कि अपने उत्कर्ष पर नालंदा देखने वालों को कितना चकित कर गया।

panels.shot: medium shot of multiple classes in different spaces
panels.action: In one frame-like view across the complex, a group studies medicine near a herb garden, another traces geometric figures on the ground, while in the shade a teacher explains subtle points of logic to a small circle.

dialogue.text: Curricula reached beyond devotion. Doctors trained with herbs and case histories, logicians tested inference, mathematicians scratched figures into dust. The mind was treated as a field to be tilled.

i18n.hi: पाठ्यक्रम भक्ति से भी आगे जाते थे। वैद्य जड़ी-बूटियों और रोगों से सीखते, तर्कशास्त्री अनुमान परखते, गणितज्ञ धूल में अंकों की रेखा खींचते। मन को जोतने की ज़मीन माना जाता था।

panels.shot: melancholic wide shot of Nalanda many centuries later, partly damaged
panels.action: Centuries later, some of Nalanda’s buildings stand broken, trees thrusting from walls, while a few monks still walk the cloisters and smoke from a distant fire hangs faintly over the horizon.

dialogue.text: In the late twelfth century, the armies of Bakhtiyar Khilji struck Magadha. Tradition speaks of a sudden end, but traces suggest Nalanda declined in phases, lingering in reduced form before silence settled.

i18n.hi: बारहवीं सदी के उत्तरार्ध में बख़्तियार खिलजी की सेनाएँ मगध पर टूटीं। परंपरा अचानक अंत की बात करती है, लेकिन साक्ष्य दिखाते हैं कि नालंदा चरणों में ढला, सिमटता गया, फिर एक दिन चुप्पी छा गई।

panels.shot: modern aerial shot of Nalanda ruins
panels.action: From above in bright daylight, the excavated red-brick ruins of Nalanda spread like a geometric pattern across the ground, with pathways and information boards visible but no roofs, only outlines of the old halls.

dialogue.text: Today only a fraction of Nalanda is unearthed. In 2016 these ruins were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site, rows of brick and stair telling of what once stood in full form.

i18n.hi: आज के नालंदा का बस थोड़ा हिस्सा ही ज़मीन से निकला है। 2016 में ये खंडहर यूनेस्को विश्व धरोहर बने—ईंटों की कतारें और सीढ़ियाँ बताती हैं कि कभी क्या शान से खड़ा था।

panels.shot: slow push-in on a contemplative statue-like image of Kumaragupta in faded glory
panels.action: In an imagined visual echo, Kumaragupta the First is seen in semi-profile against a faded backdrop of both the living Nalanda and its ruins, his expression calm, hand resting on a scroll as if passing it on.

dialogue.text: Kumaragupta the First’s grant outlived his dynasty. Nalanda shows that a ruler’s richest legacy is not in gold or walls alone, but in a place where minds from many lands may grow freely together.

i18n.hi: कुमारगुप्त प्रथम का दान उसकी वंश-रेखा से भी लंबा चला। नालंदा दिखाता है कि राजा की सबसे अमीर विरासत सिर्फ सोना या किले नहीं, बल्कि वह जगह है जहाँ दूर-दूर की बुद्धियाँ साथ मिलकर स्वतंत्र रूप से बढ़ सकें।

subtitle_i18n.en: From Gupta Vision to a Thousand Lamps of Learning
subtitle_i18n.hi: गुप्त दृष्टि से हज़ार दीपों तक
